intro: Pellegrinia dichogama, also known as the "Wild Bitter Melon," is a species of climbing plant in the Cucurbitaceae family, native to tropical regions of Central Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits of antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pellegrinia dichogama include cucurbitacins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pellegrinia dichogama is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion to treat various ailments, and it is also used in the production of herbal teas, tonics, and ointments.
